question_text: Explain the difference between mixing coloured lights (additive mixing) and mixing coloured pigments (subtractive mixing). Use an example to support your answer.
correct_answer: Additive mixing: coloured lights are mixed by adding them together. Red + blue light = magenta (brighter). Subtractive mixing: coloured pigments are mixed by absorption. Red paint + blue paint = purple/dark (darker, because pigments absorb light). Pigments work by absorbing colours, while lights work by combining wavelengths.
explanation: When you mix light, you add the colours together and get brighter colours. When you mix paints, they absorb more light, so you get darker colours.
